preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the voiceless post-alveolar affricate [tʃ] (represented here as 'ch') is documented in the context of accented speech and palatalization, where sibilants move toward palatal or affricated positions.

examples.note: Phonetic shift observed in non-native speakers who replace standard retroflex segments with affricates or palatalized variants.
Broader palatalization processes where front fricatives shifted toward post-alveolar affricates before vowels like [e] or [i].

sources: Phonological research on Cantonese-accented Mandarin errors.
